programme.desc: Deep White explores ways to research sharks to determine why sharks attack and to study innovative ways to train sharks in order to prevent future attacks. Sharks are surprisingly intelligent and able to perform complex tasks. Sharks can distinguish different signals and cues in feeding routines and they develop strong bonds with their trainers.

programme.title: Xplash Ep110

programme.title: The Great Barrier Reef: World's most amazing coral reef
programme.desc: This expedition to the Great Barrier Reef is designed to study the health and resilience of one of the world’s most amazing coral reefs. The expedition’s goal is to collect data about the world's most remote reefs and build a picture of how reefs are faring in today's changing climate.

programme.title: Sports Adventure Ep503: The Wellerbrucke Rapids… A Kayaker’s Dream
programme.desc: The Wellerbrucke rapid is a world class kayaker’s dream. Not only offering up great freestyle adventures, it also hosts the Adidas Sick-line Extreme Kayak Championships. This competition is seen through the eyes of 3-time champion Sam Sutton.

programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep110: The Ship Sinkers
programme.desc: Mothballed, de-commissioned naval vessels are toxic time-bombs, filled with PCB’s, asbestos, lead paint, oil and grease. Hundreds rust in dockyards around the world. What can be done with them? A team of Canadians has developed an innovative solution…they clean the ships...then sink them!

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: After a grueling night braving the elements, the second half of the challenge continues. The All-Stars and Space Cowboys are pushed to the limit when they are tasked with fabricating their own survival structures. Then, catastrophe strikes on the rope climbs, a timber hitch tests the group’s endurance and the day finishes off with Uncle Sam’s Misguided Children’s stress course. Will the All-Stars make it through? Only time will tell.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: The best of the best are back to compete, and Don’s serving up a heaping plate of his signature 10-part PRT. A bear crawl down a mountain, log running and two-point planks are just some of Don’s welcome gauntlet. And the cherry on top—a 5k desert run. But the contestants only have themselves to blame. Because when you’re already amongst the elite, you have to do whatever it takes to find the one who stands above the rest.

programme.title: All Stars
programme.desc: It’s go time. The mission day has come and the All-Stars have to rendezvous on a nearby island to pick up their supplies. When it comes to gear, Don pulls out all the stops to ensure mission success. From water jets and state-of-the-art fins to maximize speed, to finding a creative way to keep his team lethally armed without breaking any laws.. Don has a few surprises in store for the All-Stars.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Still reeling from a mission briefing from the CIA, the contestants are thrown into a punishment for breaking protocol. It’s safe to say the contestants are having a sobering morning. However, the contestants are given a minor reprieve when Don assigns them to some stretch PT to give them a break before the ropes course gauntlet in the afternoon.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The day begins with a powerful dose of perspective as contestants hear real-life breaching stories from American heroes—reminders of the stakes they’re preparing for. The team is then introduced to a concealed firearm headrest safe designed for rapid-response vehicle defense. Next, it’s straight into the action with a preliminary course of fire using GreenLight sights, where top performers are pushed even further with a bonus round to test speed, accuracy, and composure under pressure. The intensity ramps up during a sentry scenario featuring SABRE CO₂ non-lethal cartridges, putting their stealth and judgment to the test. But just as the dust begins to settle, a new CIA briefing drops—and it’s the most dire yet. With mission reality closing in fast, the contestants close the day with a crash course in S.E.R.E. (Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape) training, rounding out their classroom combat readiness with hard-earned grit.

programme.title: Waterfall Relaxation
programme.desc: Unwind with the sights and sounds of a spectacular cascading waterfall in Northern California. Burney falls is a spring fed waterfall that has been referred to as the "eighth wonder of the world". This new release also features scenes from Manzanita Lake in Lassen National Park, a scenic high alpine lake with abundant bird life. Relaxing waterfall sounds and bird songs make this a wonderful escape from the pressures of daily life. {[TrailerUrl:https://vimeo.com/793652147]}

programme.title: The Manhunt 1984
programme.desc: In The Manhunt, Tony Anthony stars in a relentless tale of vengeance and survival. When betrayal leaves him hunted across the frontier, a lone gunman must outwit deadly pursuers and face down ruthless enemies determined to see him dead. With tense showdowns, gritty action, and a hero who refuses to quit, this hard-hitting Western delivers a man’s fight against impossible odds.

programme.title: The Trusted Outlaw 1937
programme.desc: In The Trusted Outlaw, cowboy star Bob Steele delivers action and grit as a reformed outlaw trying to walk the straight path. But when a ruthless gang frames him for crimes he didn’t commit, he’s forced back into the saddle to clear his name. With fast-paced gunfights, high-stakes drama, and Steele’s trademark toughness, this Western proves that sometimes it takes an outlaw’s courage to bring true justice to the frontier.

programme.title: Dead for a Dollar 1968
programme.desc: In Dead for a Dollar, Gianni Garko stars as a hired gun drawn into a ruthless battle over greed and betrayal. When outlaws and schemers put a price on life itself, he rides a dangerous trail where every deal is deadly and every dollar could be his last. With brutal showdowns, shifting loyalties, and the grit of Spaghetti Western style, this hard-hitting tale proves that in the West, fortune is bought with blood.

programme.title: Abilene Town (1946) Colorized : Randolph Scott
programme.desc: Randolph Scott stars in Abilene Town, a frontier drama of change, conflict, and courage. As homesteaders and cattlemen clash over land rights, Marshal Dan Mitchell (Scott) struggles to keep the peace in a town on the brink of chaos. Torn between lawless violence, shifting loyalties, and two women pulling at his heart, the marshal must decide where he stands as Abilene’s future hangs in the balance. With vivid characters, tense standoffs, and classic Western grit, this film captures the spirit of a town caught between the old West and the new.

programme.title: Heart Of The Golden West
programme.desc: Lambert owns the trucking line that ships cattle to market. When he raises his rates Roy decides to ship the cattle on the River Boat. When Lambert and his men are unable to stop the boat, they rustle the cattle.

programme.title: Robot War
programme.desc: Robots are redefining warfare, where unmanned machines fight, adapt, and kill on the front lines. Machines are learning to kill faster than humans can react. Jason Bellini reports from a Ukrainian battlefield as the future of war arrives.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.

programme.title: Robot War
programme.desc: Robots are redefining warfare, where unmanned machines fight, adapt, and kill on the front lines. Machines are learning to kill faster than humans can react. Jason Bellini reports from a Ukrainian battlefield as the future of war arrives.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.

programme.title: America 250 Road Trip Itineraries
programme.desc: Photowalks host Jefferson Graham takes a road trip through American history. He's on a journey to take the best photos following America's founding in 1776, starting in Boston and ending in Washington, DC.

programme.title: Robot War
programme.desc: Robots are redefining warfare, where unmanned machines fight, adapt, and kill on the front lines. Machines are learning to kill faster than humans can react. Jason Bellini reports from a Ukrainian battlefield as the future of war arrives.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.

programme.title: America 250 Road Trip Itineraries
programme.desc: Photowalks host Jefferson Graham takes a road trip through American history. He's on a journey to take the best photos following America's founding in 1776, starting in Boston and ending in Washington, DC.

programme.title: Robot War
programme.desc: Robots are redefining warfare, where unmanned machines fight, adapt, and kill on the front lines. Machines are learning to kill faster than humans can react. Jason Bellini reports from a Ukrainian battlefield as the future of war arrives.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.

programme.title: Robot War
programme.desc: Robots are redefining warfare, where unmanned machines fight, adapt, and kill on the front lines. Machines are learning to kill faster than humans can react. Jason Bellini reports from a Ukrainian battlefield as the future of war arrives.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.

programme.title: Robot War
programme.desc: Robots are redefining warfare, where unmanned machines fight, adapt, and kill on the front lines. Machines are learning to kill faster than humans can react. Jason Bellini reports from a Ukrainian battlefield as the future of war arrives.

programme.title: Extinction Earth
programme.desc: Host Philippe Cousteau explores Yellowstone National Park, the site of a potential super-volcano. Fifty miles of magma lurks near the surface, and if it were to erupt, its ash would cover the earth and damage crops worldwide as well as our drinking water. Then Philippe visits Meteor Crater in Arizona, the site where a meteor once struck 50,000 years ago. We'll examine the damage it caused, and wonder if it could happen again. Then we travel to the Hawaiian Island of Maui, where powerful telescopes search the universe for meteors that might be heading our way. We explore the possibility of another meteor reaching our planet.
